competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a person but I pick them because I like them, not because they know about health (for example 'my best friend').
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name a specific person I would ask if I was unsure about a health claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name one person at home and one person at school I could ask, and I say why each one is a good person to ask about health.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name any bias indicators present in a health information source.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say a source 'looks biased' but I cannot name a specific indicator, such as who funded it, what it is selling, or who wrote it.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one bias indicator (for example, 'it is an advert') but miss other visible ones such as funding source or missing author.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name any bias indicators present in a health information source.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name every visible bias indicator in the source and distinguish strong indicators (direct sponsorship) from weaker ones (a confident tone).

criteria.criterion_statement: I can state and justify my conclusion about how much weight to give a health claim based on the bias indicators I found.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I state a conclusion ('I believe it' or 'I don't') but my reason is not connected to the bias indicators I named.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I connect my conclusion to one bias indicator but I treat every indicator as equally serious, which over-weights minor signals.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can state and justify my conclusion about how much weight to give a health claim based on the bias indicators I found.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I state a conclusion that calibrates how much weight to give the claim along a scale — strong belief, partial belief, skepticism — and I justify that weighting by pointing to the most serious bias indicator.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe a specific adjustment I have implemented in response to an observed pattern.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe an adjustment in vague terms ('I tried to be better at it') without naming what I actually did differently.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I describe what I changed but leave out when or where I made the change, so the adjustment is not specific enough to repeat.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can describe a specific adjustment I have implemented in response to an observed pattern.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I describe the adjustment with enough specificity (what, when, where, how often) that someone else could follow the same intervention.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can report the effect I observed after implementing the adjustment.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say the adjustment 'worked' or 'didn't work' but I report no observation of what actually happened.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I report an observation but I only report the data that support the conclusion I already believed, ignoring times the adjustment made no difference.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can report the effect I observed after implementing the adjustment.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I report observations across more than one occasion, including at least one occasion where the adjustment did not work, and I distinguish the effect of the adjustment from other things happening around me.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can design the conditions for sustained attention in a piece of my own work, including managing digital distractions.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I list generic 'study tips' without designing conditions that fit the particular piece of work I am doing.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I design conditions that fit the task but I do not include any specific plan for managing digital distractions, so one ping from my phone resets my focus.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can design the conditions for sustained attention in a piece of my own work, including managing digital distractions.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I design conditions that fit the task and name specific digital-distraction controls (app limits, notification settings, device location) that match the length and depth of the work I am doing.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can produce a written evaluation of how different attentional strategies interacted with different types of tasks.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I list strategies I have used but do not compare how they worked on different types of task.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I compare two strategies but I only report on one type of task, so I cannot show how the strategy interacted with the task demand.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can produce a written evaluation of how different attentional strategies interacted with different types of tasks.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I produce a written evaluation that identifies a strategy that worked well for one task type and poorly for another, and I explain why using the actual task demand (reading vs. writing vs. problem-solving).

criteria.criterion_statement: I can audit my attentional patterns across a sustained period of high-stakes work.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I track my attention for one or two sessions but the data set is too thin to see any pattern.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I track attention across several sessions but I only record when focus was good, leaving gaps on distracted days.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can audit my attentional patterns across a sustained period of high-stakes work.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I audit attention across a substantial period (at least two weeks of real high-stakes work) with consistent data on both focused and distracted sessions, and I note external factors (sleep, deadlines, environment) alongside.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can produce a written analysis identifying the conditions and strategies that most reliably support my best work.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I list strategies I used but do not tie them to evidence from my audit.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify one or two supportive conditions but my claims over-generalise from a single memorable session.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can produce a written analysis identifying the conditions and strategies that most reliably support my best work.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify conditions and strategies with explicit reference to the audit data and acknowledge cases where the pattern did not hold.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can propose specific adjustments to my workflow based on my attentional audit and analysis.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I propose generic advice ('work harder', 'focus more') rather than specific workflow adjustments.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I propose one or two adjustments but they do not directly target the conditions my analysis identified.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can propose specific adjustments to my workflow based on my attentional audit and analysis.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I propose targeted workflow adjustments that map to named conditions in my analysis and I state how I will tell whether each adjustment is working.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify at least one cognitive bias or assumption likely to be affecting my reasoning in a decision I face.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I name a cognitive bias in the abstract (confirmation bias, sunk-cost) but do not locate it inside my own reasoning on the decision at hand.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I locate one bias in my reasoning but I pick the most flattering one and miss stronger candidates present in my thinking.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify at least one cognitive bias or assumption likely to be affecting my reasoning in a decision I face.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify more than one likely bias, including at least one I would rather not see in my reasoning, and I point to specific sentences in my own analysis where the bias shows up.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe an adjustment I made to my reasoning process to reduce the bias's impact.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say I 'tried to be less biased' without describing a specific change to how I reasoned.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I describe an adjustment (for example, steelmanning an opposing view) but I apply it once and then revert to my original pattern.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can describe an adjustment I made to my reasoning process to reduce the bias's impact.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I describe a specific adjustment, apply it consistently, and show how the adjustment changed a step of my reasoning I would otherwise have taken.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate the quality of my decision-making reasoning independent of the outcome.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I judge my reasoning as good because the outcome was good, or bad because the outcome was bad (outcome bias).
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I say outcome and reasoning can come apart but my evaluation still drifts toward whichever outcome I got.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evaluate the quality of my decision-making reasoning independent of the outcome.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I give an example where my reasoning was sound but the outcome was bad (or vice versa) and evaluate both cases on the reasoning alone.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can weigh multiple types of evidence on a complex, contested, high-stakes decision I face.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I cite only one type of evidence (for example, personal experience) and ignore data, testimony, or expert analysis.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I cite multiple types of evidence but give them equal weight without discussing their relative strength.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can weigh multiple types of evidence on a complex, contested, high-stakes decision I face.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I weigh multiple types of evidence and explain why I weight one more heavily than another given the particular decision at hand.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can acknowledge irreducible uncertainty in my written position on a contested decision.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I write with false confidence, treating the decision as if the evidence settles it clearly when it does not.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I admit there is 'some uncertainty' in general terms but I do not locate where in my argument the uncertainty bites.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can acknowledge irreducible uncertainty in my written position on a contested decision.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name the specific claims in my position that would remain uncertain even if I had more evidence, and I explain why that uncertainty is irreducible rather than temporary.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can make the logic of my choice explicit in writing.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I state a conclusion and some feelings but the reader cannot reconstruct why the evidence leads to the conclusion.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I show my reasoning in parts but the link between premises and conclusion has a gap a careful reader would notice.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can make the logic of my choice explicit in writing.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I lay out my reasoning step by step so that a reader can see exactly where they would need to disagree with me to reach a different conclusion.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name what would cause me to revise my position on a contested decision.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say my view is 'strong' or 'firm' but do not name any evidence that would change it.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name evidence that would change my mind but the bar is so high that no realistic evidence could meet it.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name what would cause me to revise my position on a contested decision.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name specific, realistic kinds of evidence or argument that would shift my position, and I distinguish kinds that would strengthen confidence from kinds that would reverse it.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can analyse the structural and social factors affecting my access to healthy habits.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I locate all barriers to healthy habits as personal willpower problems and do not name any structural factors.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one structural factor (cost, time, safe outdoor space) but I treat it as a single obstacle rather than part of a wider pattern.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can analyse the structural and social factors affecting my access to healthy habits.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I analyse the interaction of structural factors (cost, time, access, safety) and social factors (family norms, peer expectations) and identify which are mine to change and which are not.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate health claims in two sources using evidence-quality criteria.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I compare two sources but my judgment rests on presentation style rather than evidence quality.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I apply one evidence-quality criterion (for example, who funded it) but miss criteria such as study design or sample size.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evaluate health claims in two sources using evidence-quality criteria.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I apply multiple evidence-quality criteria and explain which criterion is most decisive for the particular health claim being made.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can produce a redesigned habits plan that accounts for conditions I cannot individually control.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I redesign the plan by asking myself to try harder, with no change to the conditions that blocked the previous version.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I adjust the plan for one uncontrollable condition (for example, school timetable) but I ignore other conditions my analysis identified.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can produce a redesigned habits plan that accounts for conditions I cannot individually control.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I redesign the plan around the uncontrollable conditions I named, including backup strategies for days when the conditions change.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can produce a designed health maintenance approach sustainable across the demands of adult life, accounting for competing priorities, social pressures, and systems-level constraints.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I produce a health plan that would only work in a week with no competing priorities or social pressures.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I design a plan that acknowledges competing priorities but collapses under social pressure or predictable life events I should have anticipated.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can produce a designed health maintenance approach sustainable across the demands of adult life, accounting for competing priorities, social pressures, and systems-level constraints.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I design a health approach with explicit fallback positions for predictable disruptions (travel, deadlines, relationship change) and I distinguish what stays constant from what flexes.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can articulate in writing why sustainable habits require external conditions, not only personal willpower.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I claim sustainable habits are about willpower alone, or I deny that willpower plays any role.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I argue that external conditions matter but treat them as helpful add-ons rather than necessary supports.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can articulate in writing why sustainable habits require external conditions, not only personal willpower.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I argue that sustainable habits depend on external conditions and name specific mechanisms (environment design, social defaults, system constraints) by which those conditions do the work willpower cannot.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply the relevant legal framework to a coercion, grooming, or exploitation scenario (online or institutional).

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I state that 'it is illegal' without naming which law, age-of-consent rule, or safeguarding duty applies.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a relevant law but misapply it to the scenario (for example, applying adult-consent rules to a minor).
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can apply the relevant legal framework to a coercion, grooming, or exploitation scenario (online or institutional).
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I apply the relevant legal framework accurately and identify where the legal frame leaves the situation unresolved and where safeguarding duties take over.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate the institutional context surrounding a coercion, grooming, or exploitation scenario.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I treat the scenario as one-on-one, ignoring the institutional setting (school, club, online platform) that enables or constrains it.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify the institutional setting but only describe its role in general, not how it shaped this particular scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evaluate the institutional context surrounding a coercion, grooming, or exploitation scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I evaluate the institutional context including power asymmetries, reporting routes, and past handling of similar cases, and I name where institutional features made disclosure harder.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can draft a non-directive, trauma-aware response to a friend disclosing a coercion, grooming, or exploitation situation.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I draft a response that tells the friend what to do or promises to keep it secret.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I draft a response that is supportive but directs the friend toward one specific action rather than offering options.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can draft a non-directive, trauma-aware response to a friend disclosing a coercion, grooming, or exploitation situation.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I draft a response that centres the friend's agency, names safe adults and services without telling them to use one, and explicitly distinguishes support from secret-keeping.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can facilitate a 20-minute structured discussion about consent, power, and boundaries with peers or younger students using age-appropriate language throughout.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: My facilitation uses language pitched above or below the audience (jargon with younger students, or vague euphemism with peers).
competency_level_descriptors.developing: My language is mostly age-appropriate but I code-switch inconsistently when sensitive words come up.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can facilitate a 20-minute structured discussion about consent, power, and boundaries with peers or younger students using age-appropriate language throughout.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I calibrate language across the full session, introducing specific terminology where needed and pitching examples to what the audience would plausibly meet.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can surface and correct at least two common myths about consent, power, or boundaries using evidence during the discussion.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I state a correct position but do not surface the underlying myth or offer evidence.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I surface one myth and correct it but the second myth I handle goes uncorrected, or I correct without evidence.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can surface and correct at least two common myths about consent, power, or boundaries using evidence during the discussion.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I surface at least two myths, correct each with named evidence, and invite the group to engage with where the myth came from.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can close the discussion with a rights-based framing of consent, power, and boundaries.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I close with a moral instruction ('be kind') rather than a rights-based framing.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name rights in the closing but do not tie them to the specific content of the session.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can close the discussion with a rights-based framing of consent, power, and boundaries.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I close with a rights-based framing that names the specific rights covered in the session and points the group to next steps, services, or people they could turn to.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the relevant healthcare service for a reproductive or sexual-health question.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I name 'a doctor' in general rather than identifying the specific service that would handle the question (GP, sexual health clinic, pharmacy, helpline).
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a specific service but pick one that does not actually offer the relevant care for this question.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the relevant healthcare service for a reproductive or sexual-health question.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify a primary service and a backup service, and distinguish which kinds of question each can and cannot handle.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can state the access pathway and confidentiality conditions for the identified service in this jurisdiction.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I state I would 'just go' to the service without naming how to access it (appointment, walk-in, referral) or what it costs.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name the access pathway but state confidentiality in general terms ('it's private') without the jurisdiction-specific rules for minors or disclosure.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can state the access pathway and confidentiality conditions for the identified service in this jurisdiction.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I state the access pathway and the specific confidentiality rules, including where confidentiality can be breached (risk of harm, age thresholds) in this jurisdiction.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate at least two information sources on a reproductive or sexual-health question against stated evidence-based guideline criteria.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I compare two sources on presentation style or familiarity rather than against guideline criteria.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I apply one guideline criterion (for example, does the source cite a named guideline?) but miss criteria such as currency or authorship qualifications.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evaluate at least two information sources on a reproductive or sexual-health question against stated evidence-based guideline criteria.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I evaluate multiple sources against several guideline criteria and state which source I would rely on for this question and why.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can weigh the quality of evidence on each side of a contested sexual-and-reproductive-health claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I present one side's evidence only, or I treat all evidence as equally valid.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I present both sides but I do not distinguish strong evidence from weak within each side.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can weigh the quality of evidence on each side of a contested sexual-and-reproductive-health claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I weigh evidence on each side using named quality criteria and explain why a given piece of evidence settles or fails to settle the point in question.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the ideological, cultural, or commercial interests shaping the discourse around a contested SRH claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I treat the discourse as interest-free, as if only the evidence is in play.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify interests on one side but not the other, or I identify interests in general terms without naming who or what.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the ideological, cultural, or commercial interests shaping the discourse around a contested SRH claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify specific interests on each side and discuss how those interests shape what evidence gets foregrounded and what gets omitted.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can acknowledge what remains uncertain in my written evaluation of a contested SRH claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I write as if the evidence settles the claim, acknowledging no uncertainty.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I acknowledge uncertainty in generic terms but do not locate where in my argument the uncertainty bites.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can acknowledge what remains uncertain in my written evaluation of a contested SRH claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name specific claims in my evaluation that remain genuinely uncertain, and distinguish uncertainty that more research could resolve from uncertainty that it cannot.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can articulate my reasoned personal position on a contested SRH claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I refuse to take a position, or I take one without tying it to my evaluation.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I state a position and give reasons but the reasons do not engage with the strongest counter-evidence I presented.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can articulate my reasoned personal position on a contested SRH claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I articulate a position that explicitly addresses the strongest counter-evidence and states what considerations carry most weight in my conclusion.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can complete a communication task across significant difference (cultural, generational, or positional) in which I adjust my register and tone to the context without losing my own perspective.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I either fail to adjust my register (sounding out of place) or I adjust so completely that I drop my own perspective to match the other person.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I adjust register well but when my perspective is challenged I either back down or reassert without acknowledging the shift.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can complete a communication task across significant difference (cultural, generational, or positional) in which I adjust my register and tone to the context without losing my own perspective.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I adjust register consistently across the interaction while keeping my own perspective visible, and I repair when I notice the register slipping.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can demonstrate in written reflection my awareness of the power dynamics shaping who gets heard in cross-difference communication.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe the interaction without naming any power dynamic at play.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a power dynamic (age, status, cultural position) but treat it as background rather than showing how it shaped who got heard in this interaction.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can demonstrate in written reflection my awareness of the power dynamics shaping who gets heard in cross-difference communication.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify specific moments in the interaction where power dynamics shaped attention or legitimacy, including places where my own position carried weight I did not notice at the time.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can judge whether the body of evidence supporting a specific health claim warrants the confidence placed in the claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I judge a single study rather than the body of evidence, or I confuse confidence in the claim with the claim being true.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I consider multiple studies but weight all of them equally regardless of design or replication.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can judge whether the body of evidence supporting a specific health claim warrants the confidence placed in the claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I judge whether confidence is warranted by assessing replication, effect size, and coherence across studies, and I calibrate my confidence rather than treating the claim as on or off.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the commercial or institutional interests that may have shaped what research was done and published on a health claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I assume the research record is interest-free, or I suspect 'bias' without naming a specific interest.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify one interest (for example, industry funding) but miss other interests such as institutional reputation or publication bias.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the commercial or institutional interests that may have shaped what research was done and published on a health claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify interests that shaped which questions got asked, which results got published, and how findings were framed, and I point to specific evidence of that shaping.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can justify my conclusion about a health claim using peer-review, RCT-design, statistical, and funding-bias criteria.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: My justification uses one criterion (for example, 'it was peer-reviewed') as if it were sufficient.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I use two criteria but apply them loosely, without showing how the criterion actually cuts in this particular case.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can justify my conclusion about a health claim using peer-review, RCT-design, statistical, and funding-bias criteria.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I justify my conclusion using multiple criteria and explain which criterion does the most work in reaching the conclusion, and why.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can read and interpret a provided systematic review or meta-analysis at an introductory level, identifying PICO, risk-of-bias findings, pooled effect, and GRADE rating.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I summarise the review at the level of its abstract, missing PICO, risk-of-bias, pooled effect, and GRADE.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify some of the four elements (PICO, risk-of-bias, pooled effect, GRADE) but miss or misread at least one.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can read and interpret a provided systematic review or meta-analysis at an introductory level, identifying PICO, risk-of-bias findings, pooled effect, and GRADE rating.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify all four elements accurately and explain how the risk-of-bias findings and the GRADE rating together qualify the pooled-effect estimate.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can trace how a named policy recommendation or guideline was derived from its evidence base.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe the guideline but do not link it to the evidence base it claims to rest on.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I link the guideline to the evidence in general terms but miss steps in the derivation (expert judgment, value weighting, feasibility considerations).
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can trace how a named policy recommendation or guideline was derived from its evidence base.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I trace the derivation step by step and identify where the evidence alone could not determine the recommendation and expert judgment or values filled the gap.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate whether a named policy recommendation or guideline is well-supported, weakly-supported, or contested, articulating what is certain, what remains uncertain, and why.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I judge the guideline as right or wrong without engaging with the strength of its support.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I place the guideline in a support category but my articulation of certainty and uncertainty is brief or generic.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evaluate whether a named policy recommendation or guideline is well-supported, weakly-supported, or contested, articulating what is certain, what remains uncertain, and why.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I place the guideline in a support category with explicit reference to evidence strength, remaining uncertainty, and the grounds on which informed observers contest it.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can map my typical patterns in a named high-stakes academic or personal domain.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe my experience in the domain in general terms without identifying repeated patterns.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify one pattern but only name its positive instances, missing the times the pattern fails or reverses.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can map my typical patterns in a named high-stakes academic or personal domain.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I map patterns across multiple situations in the domain, including patterns that only appear under specific conditions, and I note counter-cases.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the conditions sustaining the mapped pattern.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I name the pattern but treat it as just 'how I am' rather than asking what keeps it going.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify one sustaining condition but miss the reinforcement loops (cues, rewards, social context) that keep it stable.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the conditions sustaining the mapped pattern.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify multiple sustaining conditions (cue, reward, social context, belief) and explain how they interact to keep the pattern stable even when I want it to change.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can design a specific intervention targeting the sustaining conditions of the pattern.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I design an intervention based on effort or willpower rather than on the sustaining conditions I identified.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I design an intervention that targets one sustaining condition but leaves other conditions untouched, so the pattern can route around the change.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can design a specific intervention targeting the sustaining conditions of the pattern.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I design an intervention that targets the most load-bearing sustaining condition and anticipates how the pattern might try to re-establish itself around the change.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can maintain a tracking record of the intervention's effects over a defined period.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I track only my impressions at the end, with no running record over the period.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I keep a tracking record but I only record successful days, leaving gaps on days the intervention failed.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can maintain a tracking record of the intervention's effects over a defined period.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I maintain a tracking record across the full defined period with consistent entries on both successful and unsuccessful days, and I note surrounding factors that might affect the data.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can iterate on the intervention based on what the tracking showed.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I end the cycle with a verdict ('it worked' / 'it didn't') and no iteration.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I iterate once but the change I make is not informed by the specific patterns the tracking record revealed.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can iterate on the intervention based on what the tracking showed.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I iterate in ways that directly address what the tracking revealed, and I can state what I would change again if the next cycle produced similar results.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can construct a personal metacognitive framework document — a named, explicit set of strategies, prompts, and monitoring practices I have developed.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I list strategies I have heard of without turning them into a framework that is mine and explicit.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I produce a framework but it is a loose collection rather than a structured set of strategies, prompts, and monitoring practices with clear relations between them.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can construct a personal metacognitive framework document — a named, explicit set of strategies, prompts, and monitoring practices I have developed.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I produce a framework that is structured, named, and explicit about when each strategy applies and how the monitoring practices feed back into the strategies.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evidence that I have tested and iterated on my metacognitive framework across at least two domains.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe testing the framework in one domain only, or I describe two domains but with no evidence of iteration.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I show testing in two domains but the iteration consists of small tweaks rather than changes driven by what the testing revealed.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evidence that I have tested and iterated on my metacognitive framework across at least two domains.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I evidence substantive testing in at least two domains and show at least one iteration in each that was triggered by a specific failure or limitation the testing exposed.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can articulate an application plan for how my metacognitive framework will work in the next phase of my learning and life beyond school.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say I will 'keep using' my framework without identifying what the next phase will actually demand.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I describe the next phase but my application plan is a generic extension of current practice rather than adapted to the specific demands ahead.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can articulate an application plan for how my metacognitive framework will work in the next phase of my learning and life beyond school.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I articulate an application plan that identifies specific new demands of the next phase, anticipates which parts of the framework will need to adapt, and names checkpoints for reviewing the framework over time.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain the proposed mechanism of a specific wellbeing intervention (mindfulness, exercise, breathwork, sleep-hygiene, or similar).

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe what the intervention is but not the proposed mechanism by which it would change the stress-emotion-attention-habit system.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I state a mechanism in general terms ('it reduces stress') without specifying how it changes a physiological or psychological step.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can explain the proposed mechanism of a specific wellbeing intervention (mindfulness, exercise, breathwork, sleep-hygiene, or similar).
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I explain the proposed mechanism with specific reference to a physiological or psychological pathway (for example, HPA axis, sleep architecture, reward circuitry) and name which step of the pathway the intervention acts on.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can summarise what the evidence suggests about the effectiveness of a specific wellbeing intervention.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I state the intervention 'works' or 'doesn't work' without citing any evidence.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I cite evidence but I rely on a single study or anecdote without considering whether the finding generalises.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can summarise what the evidence suggests about the effectiveness of a specific wellbeing intervention.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I summarise a body of evidence — including where effects are consistent, where they vary by population, and where claims run ahead of the evidence.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can articulate what the intervention would and would not change in the stress-emotion-attention-habit system.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I claim the intervention changes everything or nothing, rather than articulating which elements of the system it would affect.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one element the intervention would change but I ignore elements it would leave untouched.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can articulate what the intervention would and would not change in the stress-emotion-attention-habit system.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I articulate specifically which elements of stress, emotion, attention, and habit the intervention targets and which it leaves unchanged, and I explain why the targeted elements matter for the outcome claimed.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify where the evidence is strong on a contested claim in wellbeing science.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I assert the evidence is 'strong' without pointing to specific studies or features of the evidence base.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I point to one line of evidence and treat it as decisive, without considering replication or converging lines.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify where the evidence is strong on a contested claim in wellbeing science.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify the strongest parts of the evidence base and explain what makes them strong (replication, effect size, convergent methods) rather than just citing them.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify where the evidence is uncertain on a contested claim in wellbeing science.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I treat the evidence as settled on all fronts and acknowledge no uncertainty.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name general uncertainty but do not locate specific claims that remain uncertain or explain why.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify where the evidence is uncertain on a contested claim in wellbeing science.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify specific claims that remain uncertain, distinguish uncertainty due to thin evidence from uncertainty due to conflicting evidence, and explain which studies would need to change my view.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify where popular accounts have oversimplified or misrepresented research on a wellbeing claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I treat popular accounts as accurate reflections of the research, or I dismiss them all without analysis.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one oversimplification but I cannot distinguish oversimplification in media from genuine disagreement among researchers.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify where popular accounts have oversimplified or misrepresented research on a wellbeing claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify specific oversimplifications or misrepresentations in popular accounts and trace how each diverges from what the underlying research actually shows.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can state my reasoned position on a contested wellbeing-science claim with specific evidence.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I refuse to take a position, or I state one without grounding it in specific evidence from my analysis.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I state a position and cite evidence but my position does not engage with the strongest counter-evidence I identified.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can state my reasoned position on a contested wellbeing-science claim with specific evidence.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I state a position that engages explicitly with the strongest counter-evidence and identifies what weight of evidence would move me to revise.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe a situation where someone was left out or treated unkindly and name what I saw.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band A.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say 'someone was mean' without describing what actually happened or who was involved.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I describe the situation in general terms but miss a specific action or moment that marks where it became exclusion or unkindness.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can describe a situation where someone was left out or treated unkindly and name what I saw.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I describe the situation with specific detail and distinguish what I saw happen from what I guessed or imagined.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify a specific trusted adult I could tell about an exclusion or unkindness I witnessed.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band A.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say 'an adult' in general but I cannot name a specific person.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a specific adult but I hesitate or pick someone because they are near me, not because they are trusted for this kind of telling.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify a specific trusted adult I could tell about an exclusion or unkindness I witnessed.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify a primary trusted adult and a backup adult, and I can say a short reason why each is a good person to tell.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can describe what happened when someone was excluded or treated unfairly.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band B.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say 'something bad happened' without describing who did what to whom.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I describe the main action but miss details such as the setting or who was watching.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can describe what happened when someone was excluded or treated unfairly.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I describe the scenario with enough specificity (who, what, where) that another reader could picture it, and I distinguish what I saw from what I am inferring.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can suggest how the excluded or unfairly-treated person might have felt.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band B.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say the person felt 'bad' or 'sad' without considering a plausible range of feelings.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I suggest one feeling but my choice is more about what I would feel than about cues from the scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can suggest how the excluded or unfairly-treated person might have felt.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I suggest more than one plausible feeling and point to cues in the scenario (the words, the setting, the actions) that support each guess.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can select one helpful action from a short list of options with one-sentence reasoning for my choice.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band B.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I pick an option without giving a reason, or my reason is simply 'it is the best one'.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I pick an option with a reason, but the reason does not connect the action to the situation I described.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can select one helpful action from a short list of options with one-sentence reasoning for my choice.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I pick an option with a reason that connects the action to who was hurt, what was happening, and why this action fits rather than another.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the form of exclusion or discrimination present in a scenario.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say the scenario is 'mean' or 'unfair' without naming a specific form of exclusion or discrimination.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a form (for example, exclusion on gender, race, or disability) but miss that more than one form may be at play.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the form of exclusion or discrimination present in a scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name the specific form and acknowledge when more than one form is operating at the same time.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can assess whether it is safe to intervene directly in a discrimination scenario.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I decide it is or is not safe to intervene without considering who is present, how outnumbered I would be, or whether the person targeted wants my intervention.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I consider one safety factor (who is present) but miss others (the kind of harm, whether I could exit, whether the targeted person would want this).
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can assess whether it is safe to intervene directly in a discrimination scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I assess safety by weighing who is present, the kind of harm, whether I could exit, and whether the targeted person would want direct intervention or something else.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name and justify my choice from the four bystander options.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I name a choice but do not reference the four bystander options or give a reason.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name my choice from the four options and give a reason, but the reason does not connect to my earlier identification or safety assessment.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name and justify my choice from the four bystander options.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name my choice and justify it with explicit reference to the form of discrimination, the safety assessment, and what the targeted person is likely to need.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the forms of discrimination present in a bystander scenario.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I treat the scenario as simple unkindness and do not recognise discrimination.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify one form of discrimination but miss overlapping forms (for example, gender and race) in the same scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the forms of discrimination present in a bystander scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify multiple overlapping forms of discrimination and distinguish them from behaviours that look similar but are not discriminatory.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name the specific group and power dynamics at play in a bystander scenario.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe the scenario at the level of individuals only, without naming group or power dynamics.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one group or power dynamic but treat it as background rather than showing how it shaped what happened.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name the specific group and power dynamics at play in a bystander scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name specific group and power dynamics and show how they shaped who could speak, who got believed, and who had to carry the cost of the incident.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can justify a proposed response based on safety, proportionality, and what the targeted person is likely to need.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I propose a response and defend it on one criterion (usually safety) without engaging proportionality or what the targeted person needs.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I engage two of the three criteria but miss the third.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can justify a proposed response based on safety, proportionality, and what the targeted person is likely to need.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I justify my response on all three criteria and show how the criteria can pull against each other, explaining which one I prioritised and why.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can compare two or more possible responses to a discrimination scenario.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I evaluate only the response I would pick, with no comparison.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I compare two responses but I do not distinguish what each would and would not achieve.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can compare two or more possible responses to a discrimination scenario.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I compare multiple responses across what they would achieve, what they risk, and who bears the cost or the benefit of each.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify my own likely default response to a discrimination scenario and its probable sources.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe what I would ideally do, not what I would actually default to under pressure.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify a default response but treat it as purely personal, not naming its probable sources in upbringing, past experience, or group norms.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify my own likely default response to a discrimination scenario and its probable sources.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify my default response honestly, including the parts I would rather not claim, and trace it to specific sources (upbringing, peer group, past incident, media).

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain how group norms and institutional context factors shape which bystander interventions are possible or effective.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I treat bystander effectiveness as a function of individual courage, not of group norms or institutional context.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one group norm or institutional factor but do not explain how it changes which interventions work.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can explain how group norms and institutional context factors shape which bystander interventions are possible or effective.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I explain how multiple group-norm and institutional factors (what is said about incidents, how reports are handled, whose accounts get believed) change which interventions are possible in this context.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can construct a written or presented analysis of a real bystander success or failure, self-selected from my own experience or another's.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe the incident as a story without analysing the bystander dynamics at work.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I analyse the incident but my account is weighted toward what should have happened rather than what actually did.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can construct a written or presented analysis of a real bystander success or failure, self-selected from my own experience or another's.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I analyse the incident with careful attention to the sequence of choices made, the information available at each point, and the constraints the bystanders were actually operating under.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can evaluate what made effective intervention possible or impossible in a specific bystander context.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I attribute the outcome to individual courage or cowardice, ignoring contextual factors.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one contextual factor but I do not show how it specifically enabled or blocked intervention in this case.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can evaluate what made effective intervention possible or impossible in a specific bystander context.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I evaluate multiple contextual factors (group norms, power asymmetry, institutional response, visibility) and show how each factor shaped what was possible or impossible at specific moments.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can propose specific group, school, or community-level conditions that would make active bystander response more likely, with reasoning grounded in bystander research.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band F.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I propose conditions based on personal hunches without reference to bystander research.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I cite bystander research but my proposals apply it in generic terms rather than adapting it to the specific context.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can propose specific group, school, or community-level conditions that would make active bystander response more likely, with reasoning grounded in bystander research.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I propose systemic conditions that adapt named findings from bystander research to the specific context, and I state what would count as evidence that the proposed conditions were working.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can say whether an age-appropriate piece of digital content is real or made up.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band A.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say whether content is real or made up based on whether I like it, not on whether it could have happened.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I classify content but my choice switches when the same kind of content appears in a slightly different form.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can say whether an age-appropriate piece of digital content is real or made up.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I classify content consistently across a short set of examples and can say what kind of cue (cartoon style, talking animal, something impossible) helped me decide.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain my reasoning in a sentence for why I think a piece of digital content is real or made up.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band A.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I give no reason, or I repeat the content back to the teacher as if that were a reason.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I give a reason but it is about my feeling toward the content rather than about the content itself.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can explain my reasoning in a sentence for why I think a piece of digital content is real or made up.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I give a sentence-long reason that points to a specific feature of the content (something that could not happen, a drawing style, a character type).

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name a trusted adult I would ask if I was not sure about a piece of digital content.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band A.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say I would 'ask someone' without naming a specific person or role.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name a person but pick them because they are near me, not because they are trusted for this kind of question.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name a trusted adult I would ask if I was not sure about a piece of digital content.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name a trusted adult at home and at school I could ask, and I can say why each one is a good person to help me check.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the maker of each of two pieces of digital content on the same topic.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band B.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I describe the content but I cannot name who made it, even when the maker is visible.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name the maker of one piece but miss or misread the maker of the other.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the maker of each of two pieces of digital content on the same topic.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name the maker of each piece and distinguish the maker (a named person or organisation) from the platform (where it appeared).

criteria.criterion_statement: I can state whether each maker is from a trusted place and give a reason for my judgment.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band B.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say 'yes' or 'no' without a reason, or my reason is 'I have heard of them'.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I give a reason but only for one maker, leaving the second judgment unreasoned.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can state whether each maker is from a trusted place and give a reason for my judgment.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I give a reason for each maker and my reasons reference specific features of the source (who pays them, what they are there to do, whether they have to get it right).

criteria.criterion_statement: I can explain why I do or do not believe each piece of content.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band B.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say I 'believe' or 'don't believe' without a reason.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I give a reason but my reason focuses on whether the content matches what I already thought.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can explain why I do or do not believe each piece of content.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I explain my belief judgment for each piece with a reason that connects the content's claims to the kind of maker that would be in a position to know.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply lateral reading to find out who is behind each of two sources making the same claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I stay on the source's own page to decide who is behind it, rather than opening other tabs to check.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I open other sources but I only check the one that is easy to find and do not cross-check with a second.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can apply lateral reading to find out who is behind each of two sources making the same claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I apply lateral reading to both sources, cross-check with at least two independent references for each, and update my view when the references disagree with the source's self-description.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can name specific manipulation signs present or absent in each of two sources making the same claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I say a source 'seems biased' without naming a specific manipulation sign.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I name one manipulation sign (for example, emotive language) but miss others (selective framing, missing context, unsourced claims).
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can name specific manipulation signs present or absent in each of two sources making the same claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I name manipulation signs present in each source and distinguish signs that raise minor concern from signs that seriously undermine trust.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can justify which source I trust more with at least two named criteria.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band C.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I pick a source but give no named criteria, or my reason is 'it feels right'.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I use one named criterion and treat it as decisive without weighing any other.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can justify which source I trust more with at least two named criteria.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I use multiple named criteria and explain which criterion carried most weight in my decision and why.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can apply a named verification process (e.g. SIFT) to a digital claim.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I do some checking but I do not apply a named verification process, or I name one (like SIFT) without working through its steps.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I apply some steps of a named process but skip steps (for example, doing 'Stop' and 'Investigate the source' but not 'Find better coverage').
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can apply a named verification process (e.g. SIFT) to a digital claim.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I work through all the steps of a named verification process and document what each step revealed for this specific claim.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can identify the origin of a digital claim, the evidence behind it, and the motivation of the source.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I identify one of the three (origin, evidence, or motivation) but miss the other two.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I identify all three but my identification of 'motivation' stays generic ('they want clicks') without grounding in what this specific source stands to gain.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can identify the origin of a digital claim, the evidence behind it, and the motivation of the source.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I identify origin, evidence, and motivation and explain how the motivation shaped which evidence got included and which got omitted.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can justify my separate conclusions about what I would believe, what I would share, and what I would act on.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band D.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I collapse believe, share, and act into one judgment ('I believe it, so I'd share and act').
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I separate two of the three but the third judgment follows automatically from one of the others.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can justify my separate conclusions about what I would believe, what I would share, and what I would act on.
competency_level_descriptors.extending: I justify each of the three conclusions on distinct grounds, including cases where I would believe something privately but not share it, or believe it but not act on it yet.

criteria.criterion_statement: I can weigh evidence across multiple sources about a contested claim using named criteria.

competency_level_descriptors.no_evidence: The student has not produced work that demonstrates any aspect of this learning target at Band E.
competency_level_descriptors.emerging: I cite multiple sources but do not weigh them against criteria, treating each as equally authoritative.
competency_level_descriptors.developing: I apply one criterion (for example, source credibility) but miss others such as primary vs secondary sourcing or quality of underlying evidence.
competency_level_descriptors.competent: I can weigh evidence across multiple sources about a contested claim using named criteria.
